Tests the Boolean conversion of the value `false` in JavaScript.

# Purpose
This code is a simple assertion test that checks the equality between the boolean value `false` and the result of the `Boolean` function when passed `false` as an argument. The `assert` function is used to verify that the expression `false === Boolean(false)` evaluates to `true`. This test ensures that the `Boolean` function correctly converts the `false` value to a boolean type, maintaining its value as `false`. The code provides narrow functionality, focusing on a specific aspect of type conversion in JavaScript.
